metadata.title: Quick-fire: Small lies in everyday contexts
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft
metadata.design_intent: Captures System 1 honesty preferences across counterparty, stakes, and commission/omission variations. Each item is binary forced-choice under an 8-second visible timer.
metadata.domain_anchor: Abeler et al. 2019 Econometrica (truth-telling meta, N≈44,000); Fischbacher & Föllmi-Heusi 2013 die-roll paradigm; Hofmann et al. 2014 Science (mundane moral life).

items.prompt: Your housemate asks if you ate the leftovers they were saving. You did.
A friend you don't see often asks if you liked their painting. You didn't.

options.text: Say it wasn't quite your taste.

items.prompt: A customer-service agent asks if you've already used the discount code. You have.
A senior colleague asks during a meeting if you've read the doc they sent. You haven't.
An online survey, anonymous and unverifiable, asks how many hours you exercised this week. You did less than half what your aspirational answer would be.
A direct report asks if their recent work has been good. You think it's been mediocre.

options.text: Say there are some things you want to talk through.
